blocks.body: Present Simple: Questions and Short Answers — Do you...?, Does he...?, Yes, I do. / No, she doesn't.

{"level":"A1–A2","intro":"In Present Simple questions, we put do / does before the subject and use the main verb in V1.","rules":[{"label":"Do I / you / we / they ...?","color":"emerald","text":"With these subjects, start the question with do.","example":"Do you work here?"},{"label":"Does he / she / it ...?","color":"blue","text":"With he / she / it, use does.","example":"Does she like coffee?"},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","text":"In a short answer, repeat do / does or don't / doesn't.","example":"Yes, I do. / No, he doesn't."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic question models","intro":"In a question, do or does comes first.","items":[{"label":"I / you / we / they","title":"Do ... + V1?","subtitle":"Do you play tennis? / Do they live here?"},{"label":"he / she / it","title":"Does ... + V1?","subtitle":"Does he work on Fridays? / Does it open at nine?"},{"label":"Yes","title":"Yes, ... do / does.","subtitle":"Yes, I do. / Yes, she does."},{"label":"No","title":"No, ... don't / doesn't.","subtitle":"No, we don't. / No, he doesn't."}]}

{"title":"2. Why we use these questions","sections":[{"label":"About habits","color":"emerald","description":"Ask what people do regularly.","examples":[{"en":"Do you go to the gym every week?","ua":"Do you go to the gym every week?"},{"en":"Does she read before bed?","ua":"Does she read before bed?"}]},{"label":"About facts","color":"blue","description":"Ask simple questions about facts or the usual state of things.","examples":[{"en":"Does your brother live in Krakow?","ua":"Does your brother live in Krakow?"},{"en":"Do cats like water?","ua":"Do cats like water?"}]},{"label":"Short answers","color":"amber","description":"Do not repeat the whole sentence. A short model with do / does is enough.","examples":[{"en":"Do they work here? — Yes, they do.","ua":"Do they work here? — Yes, they do."},{"en":"Does he drive to work? — No, he doesn't.","ua":"Does he drive to work? — No, he doesn't."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Question and short answer","intro":"In the answer, repeat do / does instead of the whole sentence.","rows":[{"en":"Do you drink coffee?","ua":"Yes, I do. / No, I don't.","note":"do → do / don't"},{"en":"Does she work here?","ua":"Yes, she does. / No, she doesn't.","note":"does → does / doesn't"},{"en":"Do they play on Saturdays?","ua":"Yes, they do. / No, they don't.","note":"plural subject"}],"warning":"Do not say Does he works? or Yes, he do."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Keeping -s after does.","wrong":"❌ Does he works here?","right":"✅ Does he work here?"},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Leaving out do at the start of the question.","wrong":"❌ You do like coffee?","right":"✅ Do you like coffee?"},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Using the wrong form in a short answer.","wrong":"❌ Yes, he do.","right":"✅ Yes, he does."}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["A Present Simple question = Do / Does + subject + V1.","Use do with I / you / we / they.","Use does with he / she / it.","In short answers, repeat do / does or don't / doesn't.","After does, the main verb always stays in V1."]}

{"title":"Present Simple lessons","items":[{"label":"Forms and Use","url":"/en/theory/present-simple/present-simple-forms","current":false},{"label":"Negatives","url":"/en/theory/present-simple/present-simple-negatives","current":false},{"label":"Questions and Short Answers","url":"/en/theory/present-simple/present-simple-questions","current":true},{"label":"Adverbs of Frequency","url":"/en/theory/present-simple/present-simple-adverbs-of-frequency","current":false}]}
